handler_audit.evidence: src/fourcastnet/serving/inference.py: torch import guarded (L16-19); device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu' (L203-204); map_location=device (L211); .to(device) (L232,235); no hardcoded .cuda()/.to('cuda').
handler_audit.forward_requires_backend: predict_fn forward mode needs a FourCastNet backend (modulus/physicsnemo/fourcastnet) via _probe_backend (L92-115); serving/requirements.txt installs only numpy/torch/boto3, so the stock CPU image lacks the backend and forward mode returns ok=false (reason=backend_unavailable) without crashing.
handler_audit.recommended_route: JSONL S3-pointer manifest + mode=metadata_only. This loads the tensor from S3, computes metadata, returns ok=true, and does NOT require the backend.

alternative_raw_npy_route_NOT_recommende.why_not: input_fn forces mode='forward' for application/x-npy (inference.py L297), which needs the absent backend -> forward_failed. Also routes an 82MB blob through the request payload (latin1 encode path, inference.py L294) which is fragile vs S3-pointer streaming.

notes: CPU-only POC. GPU transform quota is 0; CPU (ml.m5.large) quota exists. The handler is CPU-safe and can reuse the original GPU model's model.tar.gz under a CPU image. For the FIRST canary, use the JSONL metadata_only contract above (matches data/fourcastnet_batch_request_example.jsonl and canary_payloads/canary_manifest.jsonl). DO NOT create the model or run the job as part of repo prep; this config is evidence/preparation only.
